title: The Moral Force.
content:
If thou feelest not the beautiful, still thou with reason canst will it;
    And as a spirit canst do, that which as man thou canst not.
meaning:
Friedrich Schiller's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Moral Force."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
